title: Psychotria microcephala
intro: Psychotria microcephala, also known as Makomo, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, Psychotria microcephala has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Psychotria microcephala is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used to make a tea, which is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
taxonomy: Psychotria microcephala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the genus Psychotria, which is a large and diverse group of plants that are mostly native to tropical regions. Psychotria microcephala is a member of the tribe Psychotrieae, which is a group of plants that are characterized by their distinctive flowers and leaves. The plant is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, where it grows as an understory shrub or small tree. Within the Rubiaceae family, Psychotria microcephala is classified as a species of shrub or small tree that is adapted to life in the shaded, humid environments of the tropical rainforest.

Its leaves and other parts have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
